title: Software code has mutation channels analogous to DNA, but mostly through reproduction pipelines rather than spontaneous bit flips
one_line: Program code can be modeled as a digital genome with mutation, recombination, expression, and selection, provided changes are heritable across builds/deployments.
this_is: a way to classify bugs, patches, dependency drift, and LLM-generated changes
a bridge between DNA mutation and code/version mutation
a testable hypothesis using repository history and CI/runtime logs
this_is_not: a claim that code is literally alive
a claim that every runtime bug is a genetic mutation
a claim that source files frequently mutate spontaneously
a claim that beneficial glitches are common
a replacement for ordinary software engineering explanations

formal_model.selection_operator: tests / CI / users / runtime / security / performance
formal_model.fixation_condition: mutation persists into future builds or behavior

null_case: If observed software glitches do not produce heritable changes in source/config/dependencies/models/pipeline, classify them as runtime fluctuations, not mutations.
If random or LLM-generated changes have no survival pattern beyond noise after selection, demote beneficial-mutation framing to metaphor.
If repository history shows no measurable distribution of mutation types correlated with survival/adoption, the software-DNA analogy weakens.

tests.name: Repository mutation spectrum
tests.method: Analyze commits over time; classify diffs as bugfix, feature, refactor, dependency, config, generated patch; measure which persist or revert.

tests.name: Runtime glitch fixation test
tests.method: Track incidents over one month; ask whether each incident caused persistent code/config/process change.

tests.name: LLM patch mutation test
tests.method: Generate multiple candidate patches for same bug; run tests; classify dead/neutral/beneficial mutations.

tests.name: Dependency drift mutation test
tests.method: Run same source across dependency versions; measure behavioral divergence.

decision.next_step: Create a taxonomy of observed last-month software glitches: runtime fluctuation vs heritable mutation vs beneficial fixation.

title: AI release bugs as mutation events in product phenotype
one_line: Frequent AI product releases create mutation-like behavioral changes because model weights, routing, safety classifiers, tools, UI, and policy layers interact as a single expressed genome.
this_is: a model of release-induced behavioral variation
a way to classify funny AI bugs as phenotype mutations
a bridge between software release engineering and biological evolution
a practical lens for tracking which accidental behaviors become features
this_is_not: a claim that source code physically mutates by itself
a claim that AI products are literally biological organisms
a claim that every release bug is useful
a claim that all AI companies update on an identical fixed schedule
a replacement for ordinary regression testing and incident analysis

formal_model.expression_event: release / rollout / feature flag / model routing update
formal_model.selection_event: rollback, patch, user adoption, documentation, or feature retention
formal_model.fixation: behavior persists across later releases or becomes a supported workflow

null_cases: If the funny behavior cannot be reproduced after controlling for model, account, region, and rollout state, classify it as transient noise, not mutation.
If the behavior is fully explained by documented intentional release notes, classify it as designed phenotype, not bug mutation.
If it is immediately rolled back and leaves no process/product trace, classify it as dead mutation.
If no release/config/tool/classifier change correlates with behavior onset, do not attribute it to release mutation.

tests.name: Release phenotype ledger
tests.method: Track observed behavior changes against release notes, model picker changes, tool availability, safety behavior, and account entitlement changes.

tests.name: Beneficial bug fixation test
tests.method: For each funny bug, check whether users adopted it and whether the company retained, documented, or refined it.

tests.name: Layer attribution
tests.method: Classify likely origin: model weights, prompt/policy, safety classifier, router/fallback, tool layer, UI, billing/entitlement, memory/retrieval.

decision.next_step: Build a one-month ledger of AI release anomalies: date, product, observed phenotype, suspected layer, reproducibility, rollback/fixation.

title: Probability changes character across physical, generative, and agentic layers
one_line: Quantum probability is constrained by amplitude geometry; molecular probability concentrates into macroscopic law; AI and human probabilities unfold as path-dependent trajectories shaped by context and attention.
this_is: a layer map of probability regimes
a bridge between quantum probability, statistical mechanics, LLM generation, and human action
a candidate lens for R07 deformations of probability and R08 orthogonality/pixel
a way to explain why 3D matter feels more coupled than AI text generation
this_is_not: a claim that quantum measurement and AI token sampling are physically identical
a claim that human decisions are controlled by a single synapse
a claim that probability is literally an external signal
a replacement for quantum mechanics, statistical mechanics, ML sampling theory, or neuroscience

quantum.name: bound probability
quantum.intuition: probability as brightness of allowed projections

llm.name: trajectory probability
llm.intuition: small token choices can redirect the semantic trajectory

human.name: embodied trajectory probability
human.intuition: small perturbations matter mainly near attractor boundaries

null_cases: If LLM output trajectories under controlled sampling show no path-dependence beyond final-token variance, the trajectory-probability analogy weakens.
If human choice experiments show no increased sensitivity near decision-boundary/metastable states, the attractor-boundary framing weakens.
If quantum probability can be mapped to a single classical sample space for incompatible observables, the bound-probability distinction fails.

tests.name: LLM path-dependence test
tests.method: Run identical prompt with controlled first-token perturbations; measure semantic divergence after N tokens.

tests.name: Human attractor-boundary analogy
tests.method: Use decision tasks with near-equal options; measure whether small primes/attention shifts disproportionately alter choices.

tests.name: Molecular concentration demo
tests.method: Simulate random walks or gas-like ensembles; show variance shrinks relative to macro observable as N grows.

decision.next_step: Split into R07 probability-deformation card and small Colab demo comparing concentration vs trajectory divergence.

title: Attention as probability-shaping operator
one_line: Attention does not equal probability; it selects the question, weights relevance, narrows the sample space, and renormalizes what outcomes become likely.
this_is: a cross-layer model connecting attention and probability
a CCT lens for quantum measurement basis, LLM attention, and human action selection
a way to explain why changing attention changes perceived and generated likelihoods
a candidate bridge for R07 deformations of probability and R08 orthogonality
this_is_not: a claim that consciousness collapses quantum states
a claim that transformer attention is identical to Born probability
a claim that attention creates reality from nothing
a replacement for formal probability theory, neuroscience, or ML mechanics

formal_sketch.interpretation: attention changes effective measure by relevance-weighting and renormalization

layer_map.quantum: measurement basis / POVM selects distinguishable outcomes; Born probabilities depend on that basis
layer_map.statistical: chosen macrovariables/coarse-graining define what probabilities are counted
layer_map.llm: attention weights route context information and shape logits before token probability
layer_map.human: attention allocation changes salience, memory access, action policies, and decision basin selection

null_cases: If changing attention/context in LLMs does not change token/action distributions under controlled conditions, the attention-as-probability-shaper model weakens.
If human attention manipulations do not alter choice probabilities in near-boundary tasks, the action-probability framing weakens.
If quantum probabilities are independent of measurement basis, the basis-as-question analogy fails.

tests.name: LLM attention/context perturbation
tests.method: Hold prompt objective constant; alter attention anchors/context salience; measure output distribution shift.

tests.name: Human salience-choice perturbation
tests.method: Near-equal decision tasks with attentional primes; measure probability shift across options.

tests.name: Sample-space reframing test
tests.method: Ask same problem under different framing; compare perceived probabilities and selected variables.

decision.next_step: Split into a formal note: attention as Radon-Nikodym-like reweighting of a base measure, with separate ML/human/quantum caveats.
